projects.name: Indian Public Policy Directory
projects.short_description: A searchable directory of AI tools, dashboards, and projects built for Indian public policy.
projects.description: A curated, searchable directory of AI-powered tools, data dashboards, and open-source projects relevant to Indian public policy.

projects.name: India Semiconductor Manufacturing Tracker
projects.short_description: Tracking fabs, ATMP facilities, and compound semiconductor units under the India Semiconductor Mission.
projects.description: A live tracker of semiconductor manufacturing investments in India covering fabrication plants, ATMP facilities, and compound semiconductor units under the India Semiconductor Mission.

projects.name: Parliament Committee Watch
projects.short_description: Track and search Indian Parliamentary Committee reports from sansad.in.
projects.description: A Streamlit app that aggregates and makes searchable the reports published by Indian Parliamentary Standing and Select Committees on sansad.in.

projects.name: Frameworks for Public Policy
projects.short_description: 99 public policy frameworks explained — browse by category or describe a problem to find the most relevant one.
projects.description: A reference site for public policy frameworks drawn from 300+ editions of Anticipating the Unintended, a newsletter on Indian public policy.

projects.name: China-US Rhetoric Tracker
projects.short_description: Tracking People's Daily rhetoric toward the US, visualized over time.
projects.description: A data visualization project tracking how People's Daily has covered the United States over time. Useful for analysts studying shifts in Chinese foreign policy signalling.

form.question: How to support
form.response:
1. Add projects to the AI for Public Policy directory on aipublicpolicy.org
2. Contribute PRs
3. Fork and improve the projects

form.question: A small brief about your project
form.response: I am building tools using LLMs to improve public policy discourse in India

form.question: One FOSS maintainer lesson for your younger self
Why do you do it? Why do you bother maintaining a FOSS project?
form.response: Because it's fun! It's a frictionless way of being useful.

form.question: If your repo had a theme song, what would it be?
Which file in your project would you most like to set on fire?
form.response: To see people use the parliamentwatch repo to access the excellent parliament standing committee reports, and ask tough questions of the executive based on them.

form.question: What's your open-source villain origin story?
If you had to use one emoji to convey what it is like to be a FOSS maintainer, what would it be?
